frameworks.name: continuous attractor network
frameworks.one_line: memory module sustains representation via continuous attractor dynamics

frameworks.name: efficient sensory coding
frameworks.one_line: sensory module encodes orientation matched to natural statistics

frameworks.name: discrete attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: two-module coupling yields discrete attractors with drift and nonuniform diffusion

frameworks.name: single-module memory model
frameworks.one_line: fails to capture jointly evolving bias and variance patterns

core_question: What network architecture can simultaneously produce evolving repulsive bias and minimum variance near cardinal orientations during visual working memory?
key_assumptions: orientation tuning in the sensory module reflects natural-image statistics
inhibitory modulation shapes heterogeneous sensory representation
memory maintenance and efficient encoding cannot coexist in one module
manipulations: module connectivity (sensory-only vs memory-only vs fully connected)
orientation stimulus relative to cardinal vs oblique axes
key_findings: repulsive bias and minimum-variance patterns near cardinal axes continuously evolve over the delay
single-module networks cannot reproduce these evolving error patterns
two-module sensory-memory network forms discrete attractors with moderate drift and nonuniform diffusion
sensory module shows heterogeneous tuning with strong inhibitory modulation
interpretations: sensory-memory interaction is required to continuously shape stimulus representation during working memory
modular separation lets a network satisfy efficient encoding and stable maintenance simultaneously
